transcript.clean_text: after an early establishment of hierarchy on yesterday's opening time trial today we had the first mass start road stage at the 104th giro d'italia 179 kilometers the total distance between the start town of stupenige and the finish line located in navara predominantly flat but with one categorized bump just before the mid point of the stage the first at the top of that climb would guarantee themselves the first blue climbers jersey of the race just along as they made it to the finish that's where we expected to see a big bunch sprint with caleb ewan tim malia and dylan grunervegan amongst the favourites at the start today the peloton and everybody else paid their respect to the late walter waydens the belgian rider tragically passed away on this day 10 years ago after an incident in this very race once they got going it wasn't long before we had our first attack of the day and it was the first one that stuck three italian riders on italian teams filippo taliani of android catalyst the man who took the initiative and he was followed by umberto morenga abadiani csf and in the best place rider in the gc vincenzo albanese of the yolo committee team 102nd spot 54 seconds behind filippo ghana it was a fiercely fought battle as you'd expect to crash the only climb of the day in front albenet was the first to kick and try as he might tabliani was unable to come around his countrymen before the line soon after albanese suffered an untimely or perhaps timely mechanical problem his job effectively done for the day so after a slow wheel change and an eventual swapping of bikes the 24 year old decided to sit up and wait for the bunch behind and so we were left with two tabliani taking the maximum points at the first of the intermediate sprints whilst behind there was a bit of confusion in the fight for the remaining points viviani thought he'd got them but that was the 40k to go sign so in the end it was gaviria who took the first of the main peloton and the points there at the second intermediate sprint there were bonus seconds on offer and with the two-man breakaway swept up they were all up for grabs attack is the best form of defense as they say and it was the malia rosa philippo gander who took the three bonus seconds with avanipo just behind him a move that saw him move up three places on the gc to fourth and so we rallied ourselves to what was said to be a fast frantic and chaotic bunch sprint into the closing kilometer sabatini was leading out elia viviani once his job was done milano took over on behalf of fernando gaviria at this point viviani glued to his wheel but before the italian had even started his sprint timberlea of alps in fenix was off he likes to go long and that's exactly what he did again today giacomo nizola on his wheel but unable to get past before the finish line first grand tour and first stage win for tim malia of alps in fenix if we look back now at the overhead shots we see that fernando gaviro went the wrong side of his lead out man milano thank goodness the barriers held up today which allowed him to do exactly the same thing dylan gruner vega was very impressive given that this is his first race back in nine months but that top end isn't quite there yet so he would have to settle for fourth place behind viviani in third behind the top four segan weaved and bumped his way through to fifth on the day moshetti fiorelli narsen and tim alive behind him and we had disappointed caleb tonight he never really got himself into the right position and 10th was all he could do on the day here's a man who will be happy though felipe not only conserved his lead he extended it he's now 13 seconds clear of athena's second 16 in front of foss in third whilst it's the current quick step dure of avon pool and almeida who are now fourth and fifth respectively let's take a look at what's coming up tomorrow then a three-star stage means we have a bit more climbing 190 kilometers starts in bl and it gets interesting from the first intermediate sprint after 114 k's because from there straight onto a third category climb two further climbs come in the following 30 kilometers and then the final sprint is also located atop an unclassified climb that one comes with 15ks to go to the finish line in canali sprint attack or breakaway let us know what your predictions are for tomorrow i'm going for peter sagan see you then you
